findings.notes: Helper method that logs environment variables, command line arguments, and system details during startup
The Startup.cs file defines the entrypoint for the Jellyfin server application, configuring ASP.NET Core services and middleware.
The controller handles startup wizard functionality including configuration, remote access, and user setup in Jellyfin API.
The StartupOptions class is used by the CommandLine package to parse command-line arguments and configure Jellyfin server behavior.
A C# class implementing ITaskTrigger that triggers actions during application startup with a 3-second delay, relevant to scheduled task execution in Emby's service.
A C# implementation for comparing media items by runtime duration, used in Emby's sorting logic.
A sorting comparer implementation in Emby Server for comparing items by start date, used in Live TV program sorting logic.
A C# class used to define log topics for the Jellyfin Server Startup UI, likely used to structure and display startup logs in a hierarchical format.
A C# extension method used to register the startup logger service in the Jellyfin.Server application, indicating potential involvement in application initialization and logging setup.
A DTO class used to define startup configuration parameters in Jellyfin API, potentially used for configuration input during application startup.
Defines possible startup modes for Jellyfin Server, used to configure runtime behavior such as normal operation, system migration, or database seeding.
A data transfer object used in Jellyfin API to represent user credentials during startup, likely used for authentication or configuration purposes.
A DTO class used in Jellyfin API for configuring remote access settings during startup.
Configures direct playback profiles for various media types (audio, video) with supported codecs and containers
Indicates the main implementation module for Emby Server, relevant for snap packaging entry points
The profile is specifically tailored for WebOS devices, indicating platform-specific behavior in media transcoding and playback.
Defines direct play capabilities for various media types and codecs, indicating supported playback formats for the Chrome device.
Configures transcoding behavior for audio and video, including streaming and static contexts with specific codecs and protocols.
Indirectly referenced through application context; primary entrypoint for Emby server service
Specifies video codec behavior based on conditions like video profile, level, and interlacing, used for hardware compatibility and encoding decisions.
Defines a format string for displaying app and device names in the UI, indicating dynamic content used in user-facing messages.
Config file used for probing video stream metadata in WebM format, relevant for media encoding testing in Jellyfin.
Config file used for probing interlaced video streams in Jellyfin MediaEncoding tests
The main executable path used in launch configurations for .NET Core launches
Specifies the path to the Armenian language localization file used in Emby Server
Config file used to provide localized strings for Emby Server UI in Zulu
Config file used to provide localized strings for UI elements in Icelandic
The task runner uses 'dotnet' as the command to execute build and test operations, indicating a dependency on the .NET SDK for building and testing the Jellyfin application.
All profiles use 'Project' as the commandName, indicating the launch is tied to the project's default entry point in the development environment.

Indicates a message type for maintaining server connectivity, possibly used in HTTP server lifecycle management.

Indicates the test suite entry point for validating Live TV scheduling and station data retrieval.
The entry point of the Jellyfin.Server application is defined in the Program.cs file, which is invoked via the Main method when the application starts.
Extends the web host builder to configure the application's hosting environment.
The method responsible for extracting keyframes using ffprobe, which is a key component in the media encoding pipeline.
Assembly name used as entry point for the server application

Determines whether Emby should run as a background service (indicated by --service command line option).
Specifies the package name used in the Emby server configuration, likely for identification or metadata purposes.
